commit.message:
fix: wrap optional `age` in guard block to fix optional-nested test (#133)

* fix: wrap optional `age` property in guard block in optional-nested template

The `age` property is marked as `optional` in the Concerto model but was
used directly as `{{age}}` without an `{{#optional}}` guard. After PR #53
introduced stricter compile-time checks for optional properties, this
causes the test to fail with: "Optional properties used without guards: age"

Wraps the bare `{{age}}` reference in `{{#optional age}}{{this}}{{/optional}}`
to align with the new validation rules.

Fixes #112

* Apply suggestion from @Copilot

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: nav layout broken — replace Tailwind custom tokens with LANDING_CSS classes

Root cause: bg-sand-100/90 and other custom token opacity modifiers
didn't generate in production Tailwind build. Nav appeared unstyled.

Fix: moved all nav styles to LANDING_CSS constant with raw CSS —
background, flex layout, gap, responsive show/hide, hover states.
No Tailwind custom token dependency for nav.

commit.message:
Implement soft-delete, add Trash tab and purge modal, add deleted_at migration, improve admin accessibility and indeterminate checkbox, make review upload robust

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Merge pull request #26 from dchu3/fix/cli-input-cursor

fix: use useReducer for atomic cursor/value updates and split delete/backspace

Fixes from multi-model review:
- Stale closure: replace separate useState for value+cursor with
  useReducer so all mutations are atomic (no batched-input desync)
- Delete key: split backspace/delete into separate handlers; delete
  removes char at cursor without moving, backspace removes before cursor
- Down arrow: only restore draft when leaving history (index 0→-1),
  ignore when already at -1

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix zoxide/delta install to use actual release asset URLs

The .deb filename pattern was wrong for zoxide (expected
zoxide_VERSION_ARCH.deb but actual is zoxide_VERSION-1_ARCH.deb).
Instead of guessing filenames, extract the browser_download_url
directly from the GitHub releases API for both delta and zoxide.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix ev formulas (#52)

* fix: improve EV formulas with one-level lookahead

Check EV now models villain continuation (check-back vs bet) with hero
optimally calling or folding when facing a bet, instead of the simplistic
equity * pot formula that assumed immediate showdown.

Bet/raise EV now allows hero to call villain's raise when profitable,
instead of always assuming hero folds (which made raise_branch = -bet).

Extracted compute_check_ev() and compute_aggressive_ev() as testable
helper functions with 13 unit tests covering edge cases and formula
correctness.

Closes #49

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: compute postflop raise sizes as multiplier of villain's bet

R3x/R4x/R5x were calculated as 300-500% of pot, resulting in
massive overbets (12-20x villain's bet) that gave hero excessive
fold equity and made calling never optimal.

Now computed as 3x/4x/5x the villain's bet amount, producing
realistic raise sizes (e.g. R3x facing a 33% cbet = 74% pot
instead of 300% pot).

Also fixed pot calculation in the router to handle raises
consistently when they appear in action history.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add pot/sizing debug stats to solver output

Shows pot size (BB), call amount (BB), bet size (BB + %pot) for
each action in the debug panel. Helps diagnose solver issues.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: correct pot double-counting in raise EV formula

When hero raises over villain's bet, pot_bb already includes the
villain's prior bet (call_amount_bb). The formula was using
pot_bb + 2*bet_bb which double-counts call_amount_bb.

Fixed by subtracting call_amount_bb from both the call branch
and re-raise branch pot totals. Added call_amount_bb parameter
to compute_aggressive_ev() (defaults to 0 for open bets).

Added test verifying correct pot accounting for raise-over-bet
scenario (R5x facing B33: 23.65 BB not 25.465 BB).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add SPR-aware future value to EV calculations

On early streets, the solver now estimates additional EV from remaining
streets when villain commits chips (calls a bet or bets after a check).
This makes smaller bets more attractive for value hands because they
keep villain in for future streets.

Key mechanics:
- Future value = (2*equity - 1) * remaining_stack * extraction_rate * spr_factor
- Higher SPR (smaller bets) -> more room for future betting -> higher bonus
- Only hands with equity > 50% get the bonus (floored at 0)
- Applied to: bet/call branch, raise/hero-calls branch, check/facing-bet branch
- NOT applied to: fold branches, check-back branch (no committed money)

Example impact (AK on K72r, 100 BB deep):
- Before: R5x (9.57) > B33 (7.48) -- solver always raises big
- After:  B33 (11.22) > R5x (10.72) -- solver prefers smaller bet

Also adds effective stack and SPR to debug panel.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* tune: reduce extraction rate and steepen SPR curve

Lower EXTRACTION_RATE from 0.15 to 0.10 to prevent over-slowplaying.
Switch SPR factor from linear (spr/10) to sqrt curve (sqrt(spr/8))
which more steeply penalises low-SPR situations where hero is
pot-committed and has minimal optionality on future streets.

Effect: B33 ≈ R5x ≈ B66 for value hands (~13 BB each), producing
a realistic mixed strategy instead of always slowplaying.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use hard splits for sub-range equity calculations

eval7 ignores combo weights, so the dampening factor (0.3) had no
effect on equity calculations — eq_vs_callers was identical to
eq_vs_full_range. This caused the solver to overbet with marginal
hands because it thought QQ's 63% equity held against callers too.

Added hard_split parameter to filter_range_by_equity(). When True,
combos only appear in their assigned bucket (for equity calc).
When False (default), soft dampening is preserved for cross-street
range narrowing so hands don't drop out too quickly.

EV calculation call sites now use hard_split=True. Range narrowing
in compute_current_ranges() keeps soft splits (hard_split=False).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: deduplicate env keys in reusable release workflow (#956)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(stable_diffusion): implement SDCppGenerator, real PNG encoder, and SDStubGenerator img2img pass-through (#4576)

- Add SDCppGenerator (sd_generator.h): wraps stable-diffusion.cpp txt2img/img2img
  C API; compiled only when THEMIS_ENABLE_STABLE_DIFFUSION is defined; SDPlugin
  default ctor uses it automatically when the lib is available.
- Fix encodeMinimalPng (sd_plugin.cpp): replace stub (IHDR+IEND, no IDAT, zero CRC)
  with a self-contained encoder using stored-deflate zlib, CRC-32, and Adler-32.
  No external deps required.
- Add SDStubGenerator::generateImg2Img override (sd_generator.h): returns input
  image data as output (pass-through) when valid input is provided; falls back to
  generate() otherwise.
- Add 6 new tests (groups P and Q, 51 total): PNG signature/IDAT/IHDR checks and
  SDStubGenerator img2img dimension/data/fallback verification.
- Update ROADMAP.md: mark completed items, update test count to 51.

commit.message:
Replace AmidiTransport with persistent DryWetMidi connection

- Implement DryWetMidiConnection with persistent input listener,
  address-keyed TCS reply correlator, and PushNotificationReceived event
- Switch DefaultMidiTransport to always use DryWetMidiTransport on all platforms
- Add PushNotificationReceived to IMidiConnection and IKatanaSession
- KatanaSession forwards push events and writes EDITOR_COMMUNICATION_MODE=1
  on connect (tells amp to send unsolicited DT1 push notifications) and 0 on disconnect
- MainWindowViewModel subscribes to push notifications, builds an address-to-action
  lookup at connect time, and applies push values via ApplyDeviceState to update
  the UI without echo-writing back to the amp
- Document BTS architecture, reply correlator, and state update pattern in TECHNICAL_NOTES.md

Each RQ1→DT1 round-trip now takes the actual device latency (~100-200ms)
instead of the amidi recording duration (2s). Full initial load drops from ~30s to ~2-3s.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Guard: no config → always redirect to federation-setup

Three layers of protection:
1. SessionStart hook: checks federate.config.json first. If missing, ALL
   federation requests route to setup wizard.
2. federation-orchestration skill: prerequisites section redirects to setup.
3. @federation agent: first instruction is check config, redirect if missing.

No matter which entry point triggers — hook, skill, or agent — a missing
config always lands on the setup wizard.

commit.message:
Fix skill trigger overlap — setup vs orchestration

federation-setup: triggers on creation intent (go multi-team, I need teams,
federate this project). Explicitly notes: only when no config exists yet.

monitor, send directive, sync skills). Only when config already exists.

that were catching setup-intent phrases.

feat: import 15-min consumption values as recorder statistics

Each day's 96 x 15-min values are imported into recorder with timestamps.
User can view full 15-min history in Entwicklertools → Statistiken.

refactor: split enable-auto-merge-on-pr into approve-pr and enable-auto-merge-on-pr (#58)

Separate concerns by splitting the combined action into two focused actions:

- enable-auto-merge-on-pr: only enables auto-merge with configurable merge
  method, repo setting validation, and dry-run support. Uses github.token
  from context instead of requiring a GitHub App token.

- approve-pr: new action that approves a PR using a GitHub App identity
  via actions/create-github-app-token and gh pr review --approve.

commit.message:
fix(service): use replaceView for seamless background/foreground video

Previously, backgrounding the app triggered surfaceDestroyed() which
called stopPreview(), killing Camera2 capture. On return, startPreview()
created a new RtmpCamera2/SrtCamera2 instance, orphaning the old one
with the active connection. Result: frozen video, audio continued.

Now StreamingService uses replaceView during active streams:
- detachPreviewSurface(): calls replaceViewWithBackground() to switch
  to headless mode (camera keeps capturing, encoder stays connected)
- attachPreviewSurface(): calls replaceView(openGlView) to hot-swap
  back to the new surface without interrupting the stream

stopPreview() is still used when no stream is active.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: StreamCaster-android
repository.description: StreamCaster is a native Android app for live streaming camera and/or microphone to RTMP/RTMPS endpoints.

commit.message:
feat(service): add replaceView methods to EncoderBridge for background streaming

Add replaceViewWithBackground(Context) and replaceView(OpenGlView) to
the EncoderBridge interface, wrapping RootEncoder's Camera2Base.replaceView()
API. This enables seamless background/foreground transitions:

- replaceViewWithBackground(): switches to headless GlStreamInterface,
  camera keeps capturing and encoder stays connected
- replaceView(OpenGlView): hot-swaps back to a visible surface mid-stream

Implemented in RtmpCamera2Bridge, SrtCamera2Bridge, and StubEncoderBridge.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: flip playlist dropdown to open downward when near top of screen

Measures button's getBoundingClientRect().top on open; if < 200px from
top of viewport the menu uses top: calc(100% + 8px) instead of
bottom: calc(100% + 8px), preventing it from being hidden behind the
search bar on top search result rows.

near top of screen.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add Android support, improve level difficulty and audio

- Add Capacitor for Android APK builds (app ID: com.drunkenpanda.game)
- Level 2: new music with minor scale, driving bass pulse and hi-hats
- Level 2: high-frequency waves + no smoothing for dramatic gate jumps
- Level 3: same no-smoothing treatment for varied Y gaps
- Level 3: laser now sweeps slowly top/bottom so player can dodge
- Level 3: add glowing cyan moon to neon city sky
- Track generator: configurable waves[] and smoothing per level
- Track generator: pre-warm filter to eliminate flat lead-in
- Update README with Android build and deploy instructions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: copilot session backup 13-Apr-2026

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: dark mode Phase 1 complete + Phase 2 focus/transition sweep across 20 components

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add detailed logging to token refresh endpoint to diagnose periodic reload

The FusionAuth React SDK's shouldAutoRefresh is triggering a re-login
redirect cycle whenever POST /app/refresh returns 401. Structured logs
will now capture:
- Whether a refresh token was issued at all after login (callback)
- The exact FusionAuth error body when the refresh token grant fails

This is a diagnostic step. The root fix is in FusionAuth application
settings:
1. Tokens tab: enable 'Generate Refresh Tokens'
2. JWT tab: set access token duration to ≥3600s (currently ~78s)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(multiplayer): fix lobby presence and start game navigation (Epic 18)

Story 18.1: buildConnectedPresence() now constructs LobbyPresence entries
for players not in the initial snapshot instead of discarding them. Known
players still use DB snapshot values; unknown players use self-reported
Presence payload values and sort after known players. Add full unit test
suite for presence.ts.

Story 18.2: handleStartGame() calls router.push() directly after the
game_started broadcast so the host no longer depends on a self-broadcast
round-trip. startGame() in hostActions.ts now only revalidates /play,
removing the harmful lobby-path revalidation that raced against broadcast
navigation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: integrate robot controls into existing dashboard sidebar

- Create RobotControls component (state badge, control buttons, QR phone pairing)
- Add import to CameraAskAI.tsx sidebar
- Remove /robot route from App.tsx (all controls on /dashboard)
- Fix kiosk service URL to point to /dashboard

  snapshot_id → snapshot_info.snapshot_path

- some_test_file.py: Replace undefined mod_registry with pytest.mark.skip stub

Add setup flow diagram to README before Quick Start

Shows the full chain from install to first running team: core setup →
archetype selection → archetype install → archetype setup → MCP/telemetry →
cast meta-squad → onboard first team → launch. Each layer owns its config.
